content.claim: The effect of early tracking on average (mean) student performance is contested: some studies find negative effects, others find no significant effect depending on which international assessment pairs are compared; by contrast, the inequality effect is more consistently replicated across studies.
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Disagreement reflects genuine methodological sensitivity to assessment choice and time periods
Meta-analysis results may reflect publication bias toward inequality findings
Unclear whether performance contestation applies to Hungarian context specifically
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
